name: Rahima Foundation
mission: Today, the world celebrates vast technological advancements, yet every 9th person suffers from food scarcity (1). The rate of food insecurity in the United States is even higher, standing at 11.8% (2). In the Silicon Valley, specifically, that rate more than doubles to 24.8%—making every 4th person food insecure (3). A total of 720,000 people out of 2.9 million Bay Area residents live with food insecurity (3).
theoryOfChange: Rahima Foundation states it provides a safety net to low-income community members through monthly groceries, rent and utility assistance, hot meals, and educational support, with a mission of creating equitable, resilient and inclusive communities starting with access to nutritious food for all.
scoreSummary: RAHIMA INTERNATIONAL FOUNDATION shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Rahima Foundation explicitly lists "Zakat" under its programs and services. For example, their website states, "WHAT ELSE ARE WE DOING? ... Zakat." and "OUR PROGRAMS ... Zakat." Another source confirms, "The programs that Rahima Foundation offers: food distribution, soup kitchen, meat collection, zakat and sadaqa collection, student needs." Additionally, a charity detail page for Rahima International Foundation (a 501(c)3 Charitable & Educational Foundation based in the US) lists it as "Zakat eligible." (Source: https://www.rahima.org/general-donations-rahima)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 65 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: Claims 9 geographic areas but no foreign grants on Schedule F
keyConcerns.detail: Organization lists international operations but IRS filings show no foreign grant activity.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions exceptional financial efficiency and Zakat eligibility as strengths, but doesn't adequately address the significant risk deductions due to the small board size and excessive reserves, which contributed to the moderate overall score.

all_citations.claim: The charity holds a 96.0/100 score with a 95.3% program expense ratio and 65.0 months of working capital.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and follows specific asnaf categories for distribution.
Total revenue for the period was $4,100,385, supporting programs like food distribution and soup kitchens.
The foundation offers various donation options and giving methods for supporters.
The organization provides volunteer opportunities to engage the community in its mission.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and Zakat eligibility, balanced against a need for more robust external impact verification.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing third-party impact reporting to provide more granular data on long-term beneficiary outcomes.
Increasing public transparency regarding specific program metrics to improve data confidence scores.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows medium donor fit for the Muslim community as a Zakat-eligible institution addressing the urgent cause of domestic poverty [2]. It provides multiple engagement pathways, including volunteer opportunities and structured donation options [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates direct service delivery with a program expense ratio of 95.3% and a Charity Navigator score of 96.0/100 [1][3]. While the cost per beneficiary is approximately $1,616, the foundation maintains high financial health with 65.0 months of working capital [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Rahima International Foundation is a high-impact nonprofit dedicated to alleviating domestic poverty through food security and educational support.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 95.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach those in need.
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised.
Strong liquidity position with 65.0 months of working capital.
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in California, Rahima International Foundation focuses on feeding the hungry and assisting financially insecure individuals through food distribution and soup kitchens [1][3]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [2][4].

alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="16">clarify its zakat allocation policy</cite> to provide more detail on how funds are partitioned.

credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should expand its <cite id="14">board of directors</cite> beyond the current two members to ensure better oversight.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize extreme financial efficiency and want their Zakat to support local Bay Area Muslim-led poverty relief efforts.

Prefer supporting established local institutions with a 30-year track record
Value high transparency ratings from third-party evaluators
giving_considerations: The charity has a very high reserve-to-expense ratio
Governance is centralized within a very small board
Revenue has seen a slight decline with a -5.2% CAGR
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking an organization with a large, diverse board of directors or those looking for rapid-growth startups.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The charity has a <cite id="19">documented theory of change</cite> and verified outcomes, though it lacks independent RCT evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation provides direct nutritional and financial support to stabilize families, enabling long-term educational and economic success.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that food distribution leads to measurable improvements in community health and stability.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the charity has high reserves, its <cite id="18">revenue has declined by -5.2%</cite> over three years, suggesting a need for renewed donor support.

strengths.detail: The charity directs a <cite id="7">95.3% program expense ratio</cite> toward its services, far exceeding the <cite id="10">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.
The organization spends <cite id="8">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite>, maximizing the impact of donor contributions.
The foundation maintains <cite id="11">65.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer for long-term sustainability.

strengths_deep_dive: Rahima International Foundation's financial health is anchored by its <cite id="6">96.0/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite> and its <cite id="12">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. Its ability to maintain <cite id="11">over 60 months of operating reserves</cite> while spending <cite id="7">95.3% of its budget on programs</cite> is rare in the nonprofit sector. This is achieved through a lean operational model where <cite id="13">officer compensation is limited to $112,456</cite> relative to a <cite id="5">$4 million revenue base</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1993</cite>, Rahima International Foundation is a California-based nonprofit dedicated to <cite id="2">alleviating domestic poverty</cite> through food security and educational support. The organization operates primarily in <cite id="3">San Mateo and Alameda Counties</cite>, providing essential services such as <cite id="4">food distribution and soup kitchens</cite> to the hungry and financially insecure. Rahima International Foundation has demonstrated significant financial stability, reporting <cite id="5">$4,006,022 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. The charity maintains an <cite id="6">overall score of 96.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability and transparency. Notably, the organization operates with a <cite id="7">95.3% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that every dollar donated is directed toward its core mission. This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, as the charity relies heavily on community support and volunteer engagement. In addition to its domestic work, the foundation has demonstrated capacity for large-scale giving, including a <cite id="9">$1,000,000 grant to USA FOR UNHCR</cite> to support international humanitarian efforts.
